tasks.title: Add self-improvement types and validation to ProjectSettings (shared)
tasks.description: Add to ProjectSettings in shared: selfImprovementFrequency?: 'never' | 'after_each_plan' | 'daily' | 'weekly' (default 'never'), selfImprovementLastRunAt?: string (ISO 8601), selfImprovementLastCommitSha?: string. In parseSettings(), validate selfImprovementFrequency against the four allowed values and default to 'never'; pass through lastRunAt and lastCommitSha when present. Ensure existing projects without these fields get default frequency 'never'. Acceptance: ProjectSettings type includes the three optional fields; parseSettings(raw) returns them with valid frequency or default. Run: npm test in packages/shared (or run shared settings tests).

tasks.title: Persist and expose self-improvement settings in backend
tasks.description: In project.service.ts: add selfImprovementFrequency (default 'never'), selfImprovementLastRunAt, selfImprovementLastCommitSha to buildDefaultSettings(); add them to toCanonicalSettings() (include when present). getSettings already returns parsed ProjectSettings from store; updateSettings already accepts Partial<ProjectSettings> and persists via setSettingsInStore — ensure the store does not strip these keys (it persists the merged object). Acceptance: GET /projects/:id/settings returns the new fields; PUT /projects/:id/settings accepts selfImprovementFrequency and persists it; lastRunAt/lastCommitSha are only updated by backend after a run, not by PUT. Run: npm test in packages/backend for project.service or settings routes.
dependsOn: Add self-improvement types and validation to ProjectSettings (shared)

tasks.title: Implement git-based change detection for self-improvement
tasks.description: Implement a function hasRepoChangedSince(repoPath: string, sinceIsoTimestamp: string, sinceCommitSha?: string | null): Promise<boolean>. Use git rev-list or git log --since to determine if there are commits after the timestamp (or after the given SHA). When sinceCommitSha is provided, compare HEAD to that SHA; when only timestamp, use git log --since=<timestamp>. Return true if repo has changes (or on first run when lastRunAt is missing); return false when no commits since. Handle empty repo / no git: return true so first run can proceed, or document and return false per product decision. Acceptance: unit tests with a temp git repo: no commits after timestamp -> false; commits after -> true; missing lastRunAt -> true. Run: npm test for the new or modified test file.

tasks.title: Self-improvement run state and concurrency guard
tasks.description: Create a small module or service that tracks in-progress self-improvement runs per project. Expose: startRun(projectId): boolean (returns false if already in progress), endRun(projectId): void, isRunInProgress(projectId): boolean. Use an in-memory Set or Map<projectId, runId>. Only one run per project at a time; subsequent triggers (e.g. two plans finishing close together or scheduler + plan completion) must skip or defer. Acceptance: unit test that startRun returns false when already in progress; after endRun, startRun returns true again. Run: npm test for self-improvement service tests.
dependsOn: Persist and expose self-improvement settings in backend

tasks.title: Self-improvement prompt builder and Reviewer invocation
tasks.description: In a new self-improvement.service.ts (or extend an existing module): (1) Build a self-improvement prompt that includes: codebase context (file tree, key files content or diff since last run from BranchManager/git), SPEC.md excerpt, and the project's code review lenses (review angles). When reviewAngles is empty, one general review; when 1+ angles, one run per angle (parallel where supported) with angle-specific instructions. (2) Invoke the Reviewer (or equivalent) via the existing spawn pipeline (e.g. AgentService.invokeReviewAgent or invokePlanningAgent with a file-based prompt). Write prompt to a temp or dedicated task dir (e.g. .opensprint/self-improvement/run-<ts>/prompt.md) and spawn the agent. Reuse ContextAssembler for codebase context and FinalReviewService pattern for multi-angle. Input: repoPath, projectId, settings (for lenses and agent config). Acceptance: unit test that prompt contains SPEC excerpt and lens angles when configured; integration test with mocked agent that spawn is called with correct prompt path. Run: npm test for self-improvement service.
dependsOn: Implement git-based change detection for self-improvement
Self-improvement run state and concurrency guard

tasks.title: Parse self-improvement agent output and create tasks
tasks.description: In self-improvement.service.ts: after the Reviewer run completes, parse the agent output (structured list or JSON block of improvement items: title, description?, priority?). Use extractJsonFromAgentResponse or a markdown list parser. For each item, call TaskStoreService.create(projectId, title, { description, priority, extra: { source: 'self-improvement', planId?: string } }). If output is not parseable, create a single fallback task (e.g. 'Self-improvement review failed to parse — please review agent output') or log and skip; do not crash. Emit task change events so UI updates. Acceptance: unit test for parser with sample agent output; integration test that create() is called with correct extra.source and optional planId. Run: npm test.
dependsOn: Self-improvement prompt builder and Reviewer invocation

tasks.title: Update last run timestamp and commit SHA after successful run
tasks.description: In self-improvement.service.ts: after a successful self-improvement run (tasks created or empty list returned), update project settings via ProjectService.updateSettings(projectId, { selfImprovementLastRunAt: new Date().toISOString(), selfImprovementLastCommitSha: currentHeadSha }). Get current HEAD SHA from git (e.g. BranchManager or exec git rev-parse HEAD) in the project repo. On Reviewer failure or timeout, do not update last run. Acceptance: integration test that after a successful run, getSettings returns updated selfImprovementLastRunAt and selfImprovementLastCommitSha; after a failed run, they are unchanged. Run: npm test.
dependsOn: Parse self-improvement agent output and create tasks

tasks.title: Hook self-improvement into plan execution completion
tasks.description: In merge-coordinator.service.ts: when an epic is closed after plan execution (i.e. after runFinalReviewAndCloseOrCreateTasks closes the epic, or after the last task merge when all impl tasks are closed and epic is closed), call a new hook: e.g. selfImprovementService.maybeRunAfterPlanComplete(projectId, planId). Inside that: get settings; if selfImprovementFrequency !== 'after_each_plan' return; if isRunInProgress(projectId) return; run change detection (hasRepoChangedSince); if not changed return; startRun(projectId), then run the self-improvement review (prompt + spawn + parse + create tasks), then update last run timestamp, then endRun(projectId). Do NOT trigger when the user clicks Execute (shipPlan); only when the plan's execution is fully complete (all tasks done and merged, epic closed). Acceptance: integration test that when epic is closed and frequency is after_each_plan and repo has changes, self-improvement run is triggered; when no changes, it is not; when user clicks Execute (shipPlan) only, self-improvement is not triggered. Run: npm test for merge-coordinator or self-improvement integration.
dependsOn: Self-improvement run state and concurrency guard
Self-improvement prompt builder and Reviewer invocation
Update last run timestamp and commit SHA after successful run

tasks.title: Daily and weekly self-improvement scheduler
tasks.description: Create a scheduler (e.g. self-improvement-scheduler.service.ts) that runs at midnight UTC (daily) and once per week (e.g. Sunday 00:00 UTC). On each tick, list projects; for each project with selfImprovementFrequency 'daily' or 'weekly', check the schedule (daily: run once per calendar day; weekly: run once per week). If due: run change detection; if changed and not isRunInProgress(projectId), startRun, run self-improvement flow, update last run, endRun. Reuse the pattern from nightly-deploy-scheduler.service.ts (setTimeout loop, runNightlyTick-style). Start the scheduler from backend index when the app starts. Acceptance: unit test with mocked time that at midnight UTC daily projects with frequency 'daily' are checked; weekly only on Sunday. Integration test that at the right time change detection and conditional run are invoked. Run: npm test.
dependsOn: Self-improvement run state and concurrency guard
Self-improvement prompt builder and Reviewer invocation
Update last run timestamp and commit SHA after successful run

tasks.title: Add selfImprovementRunInProgress to execute status
tasks.description: Add to OrchestratorStatus in packages/shared/src/types/agent.ts: selfImprovementRunInProgress?: boolean. In orchestrator getStatus (orchestrator.service.ts), set this from the self-improvement run state (isRunInProgress(projectId)). Ensure GET /projects/:id/execute/status returns it. Acceptance: when a self-improvement run is in progress, execute status includes selfImprovementRunInProgress: true. Run: npm test for backend execute route and shared types.
dependsOn: Self-improvement run state and concurrency guard

tasks.title: Project Settings UI: Self-improvement frequency dropdown
tasks.description: In Project Settings (ProjectSettingsModal or settings content), add a Self-improvement section (grouped with or near Code review). Single dropdown: 'Self-improvement frequency' with options: Never, After each Plan, Daily, Weekly. Default Never. Help text: 'When the codebase has changed since the last run, a review runs using your code review lenses and creates improvement tasks.' For 'After each Plan': 'Runs once after a plan's execution is fully complete (all tasks done and merged), not when you click Execute.' Persist via existing PUT /projects/:id/settings (selfImprovementFrequency). Optionally display 'Last run: <date>' when backend exposes selfImprovementLastRunAt. Acceptance: user can select and save frequency; help text is visible. Run: npm test in packages/frontend for settings; manual verification.
dependsOn: Persist and expose self-improvement settings in backend

tasks.title: Task list: badge or filter for self-improvement tasks
tasks.description: Ensure task list API returns task.extra (already on StoredTask). In the frontend task list, show a badge or label for tasks where extra?.source === 'self-improvement' (e.g. 'Self-improvement'). Optionally add a filter to show only self-improvement tasks. Acceptance: improvement tasks created by the self-improvement run are visually distinguishable in the task list. Run: npm test in packages/frontend for task list components.
dependsOn: Parse self-improvement agent output and create tasks

tasks.title: Execute phase: show self-improvement run indicator
tasks.description: In the Execute phase or execute status area (e.g. ProjectShell or ExecutePhase), when execute status includes selfImprovementRunInProgress: true, show a non-blocking indicator (e.g. 'Self-improvement review in progress…') so users understand why new tasks may appear. Use the existing useExecuteStatus or equivalent to read status. Acceptance: when a self-improvement run is active, the indicator is visible. Run: npm test and manual check.

open_questions: Empty repo or no git: should change detection return true (run once) or false (skip)? Recommendation: return true for first run when lastRunAt is missing; for daily/weekly with no commits, return false.
GET /projects/:id/self-improvement/history (list of recent runs) is deferred to v2 per spec; confirm no task needed for v1.
